subject: Computer Networking
title: MQTT: Messaging for IoT
description: A 60-second lesson on MQTT: Messaging for IoT.

hook.text: A tiny sensor on a farm sends temperature readings every minute. It has almost no bandwidth. MQTT is perfect.

buildup.text: MQTT is a lightweight publish-subscribe messaging protocol designed for constrained devices and unreliable networks.

discovery.text: Devices publish to topics. Subscribers get messages from topics they follow. Efficient and fully decoupled.

twist.text: MQTT's overhead is as small as 2 bytes per message. Compare that to HTTP's hundreds of header bytes.

climax.text: Smart homes, factory sensors, fleet tracking — billions of IoT devices rely on MQTT for lightweight communication.

punchline.text: MQTT sends tiny messages from tiny devices. Big impact.

quiz.question: Why is MQTT ideal for IoT devices?
options: Its minimal overhead works on constrained devices and networks
It provides the fastest file transfers available
It replaces TCP for all internet traffic
